# emma_volume (Resource)

This resource creates and manages storage volumes in the Emma platform.

Volumes are block storage devices that can be attached to compute instances (VMs) for persistent data storage. To create a volume, you need to specify the data center, size in gigabytes, and volume type (e.g., ssd, hdd).

Volumes can be created independently or attached to a compute instance during creation. You can also resize volumes (increase size only) and change attachments after creation.

## Example Usage

### Basic Volume Creation

```terraform
# Query available volume configurations for a data center
data "emma_volume_configurations" "aws_configs" {
  data_center_id = data.emma_data_center.aws.id
}

# Create a standalone volume
resource "emma_volume" "data_volume" {
  name           = "my-data-volume"
  data_center_id = data.emma_data_center.aws.id
  volume_gb      = 100
  volume_type    = "ssd"
}
```

### Volume with Attachment

```terraform
# Create a volume and attach it to a VM during creation
resource "emma_volume" "attached_volume" {
  name           = "vm-storage"
  data_center_id = data.emma_data_center.aws.id
  volume_gb      = 50
  volume_type    = "ssd"
  attached_to_id = emma_vm.my_vm.id
}
```

### Volume Resize

```terraform
# Resize an existing volume by increasing volume_gb
resource "emma_volume" "expandable_volume" {
  name           = "expandable-storage"
  data_center_id = data.emma_data_center.aws.id
  volume_gb      = 200  # Increase from previous value (e.g., 100 -> 200)
  volume_type    = "ssd"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `data_center_id` (String) Data center ID where the volume will be created, volume will be recreated after changing this value
- `volume_gb` (Number) Volume size in gigabytes, can only be increased
- `volume_type` (String) Volume type (e.g., ssd, hdd), volume will be recreated after changing this value

### Optional

- `attached_to_id` (Number) ID of the compute instance to attach the volume to
- `name` (String) Name of the volume

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp
- `data_center` (Attributes) Data center details (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--data_center))
- `id` (String) ID of the volume
- `is_system` (Boolean) Indicates whether the volume contains the operating system
- `location` (Attributes) Geographic location information (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--location))
- `project_id` (Number) Project ID owning the volume
- `provider` (Attributes) Cloud provider information (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--provider))
- `status` (String) Current status of the volume

<a id="nestedatt--data_center"></a>
### Nested Schema for `data_center`

Read-Only:

- `id` (String) Data center ID
- `name` (String) Data center name


<a id="nestedatt--location"></a>
### Nested Schema for `location`

Read-Only:

- `continent` (String) Continent
- `id` (Number) Location ID
- `name` (String) Location name
- `region` (String) Region


<a id="nestedatt--provider"></a>
### Nested Schema for `provider`

Read-Only:

- `id` (Number) Provider ID
- `name` (String) Provider name

## Import

Volumes can be imported using their ID:

```shell
terraform import emma_volume.example 12345
```

## Limitations and Constraints

### Volume Resizing

- **Size increases only**: Volumes can only be increased in size. Attempting to decrease the size will result in an error.
- **No downtime**: Volume resize operations are performed online without requiring detachment.

### Immutable Attributes

The following attributes cannot be changed after creation and will force resource replacement:

- `data_center_id`: Volumes cannot be moved between data centers
- `volume_type`: Volume type cannot be changed (e.g., from ssd to hdd)

### System Volumes

- **Cannot be deleted**: Volumes marked as system volumes (`is_system = true`) contain the operating system and cannot be deleted through Terraform.
- **Automatic creation**: System volumes are automatically created when provisioning compute instances and should not be managed separately.

### Volume Attachment

- **Single attachment**: A volume can only be attached to one compute instance at a time.
- **Automatic detachment**: When changing the `attached_to_id` attribute, the volume is automatically detached from the old instance before attaching to the new one.
- **Detachment before deletion**: Attached volumes are automatically detached before deletion.

### Volume Types and Availability

- **Data center specific**: Available volume types vary by data center and cloud provider.
- **Use configurations data source**: Query the `emma_volume_configurations` data source to discover available volume types and size ranges for a specific data center.

### Validation Rules

- **Minimum size**: Volume size must be at least 1 GB.
- **Maximum size**: Maximum volume size depends on the data center and volume type. Check the `emma_volume_configurations` data source for limits.
- **Valid data center ID**: The `data_center_id` must reference an existing data center accessible to your account.
- **Valid compute instance**: When using `attached_to_id`, the compute instance must exist and be in the same data center as the volume.
